name: stackexchange
description: Search Stack Overflow and 170+ Stack Exchange sites for Q&A on programming, sysadmin, science, and more. No API key required.
documentation: Searches Stack Exchange sites (Stack Overflow, Server Fault, Super User, Ask Ubuntu, and 170+ others) for questions and answers. Use for programming questions, debugging, how-to guides, sysadmin tasks, and best practices. Common triggers: 'how do I...', 'why does X error occur', 'best way to...', 'difference between X and Y'. Specify a site to target a domain (e.g., site='serverfault' for sysadmin, site='askubuntu' for Ubuntu-specific). Set has_accepted=true to filter for questions with accepted answers for high-quality results. Returns question scores, answer counts, tags, and top answer excerpts. Pair with web_read to fetch full answer content from the result URL.

trigger.description: Initiated by Chalie when programming or technical Q&A is needed

query.description: Search query (e.g., 'python async await difference', 'nginx reverse proxy config')

site.description: Stack Exchange site to search (e.g., 'stackoverflow', 'serverfault', 'superuser', 'askubuntu', 'unix'). Defaults to stackoverflow.

limit.description: Number of results to return (1-8). Capped at 8.

has_accepted.description: Filter to questions with accepted answers only. Good for authoritative results.

results.description: List of {title, url, score, answer_count, accepted_answer_id, tags, top_answer_body, view_count, creation_date}

_meta.description: Observability: fetch_latency_ms, site, quota_remaining, has_accepted_filter, result_count

tips: Use site='serverfault' for server/infrastructure questions, 'superuser' for OS questions
Set has_accepted=true for guaranteed authoritative answers
sort='votes' surfaces the most community-validated answers
Pair with web_read to fetch complete answer content from the result URL
The quota_remaining field in _meta shows remaining API calls (300/day without key)
